---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

# #### Vizualisation ####
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(packcircles)
library(scatterpie)
library(rstatix)
library(ggpubr)
library(purrr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(ggExtra)
library(viridis)

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)


# -------------------------------------------------------------- 
# Color definition for annotation of programs 
# --------------------------------------------------------------
Bin_colors <- c(
  "Bin_MP3"  = "#fdea44",  
  "Bin_MP4"  = "#a7d066", 
  "Bin_MP7"  = "#e9407b",  
  "Bin_MP9"  = "#d3e44c",  
  "Bin_MP10" = "#b16680",  
  "Bin_MP12" = "#973356",  
  "Bin_MP14" = "#3f0418",  
  "Bin_MP15" = "#f9e8cc",  
  "Bin_MP16" = "#6c9d1e",  
  "No_MP"    = "grey95" 
)

state_colors <- c(
  "Basal fate"                     = "#236ec4", 
  "Alveolar fate"                  = "#F7DCAD",  
  "AP1 activation"                 = "#fdea44",  
  "Angiogenic remodeling"          = "#a7d066", 
  "Inflammatory cytokines"         = "#e9407b",  
  "Mechanostransduction"           = "#d3e44c",  
  "Stress survival"                = "#b16680",  
  "Mitochondrial-stress response"  = "#973356",  
  "Genome maintenance"             = "#3f0418",  
  "Primed Alveolar"                = "#f9e8cc",  
  "EMT / plasticity"               = "#6c9d1e",  
  "Unknown"                        = "grey95"   
)



cols_fate <- c("basal"  = "#236ec4", 
               "LP"     = "#a01858",      
               "HS"     = "#f9c40c",  
               "Alv"    = "#F7DCAD")


cols_Group <- c("CreP_EARLY" = "#C0D69F",  
                "CreP_LATE"  = "#61980E")

cols_MonoGland <- c("8415_G2-3" = "#375708",  
                    "8718_D2-3"     = "#61980E",
                    "8964_G4"       = "#C0D69F")
```

```{r}
Input_Dir <- "C:/Users/Marthe Laisne/OneDrive - INSTITUT CURIE/m_laisne/Publications/2026_Laisne/Scripts/Output/sc_DRAG_Analyses"

# Load functional program assignments
anno_FP <-  read.delim(paste0(Input_Dir, "/FunctionalProgramAssignment.txt"))



seurat_rna <- qs::qread(file.path("C:/Users/Marthe Laisne/OneDrive - INSTITUT CURIE/m_laisne/Publications/2026_Laisne/seurat_rna_step7_new.qs"))

```


## Bubbles and Caviar

Functions: 
```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Circle packing of clones
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Create a circle-packing layout where each clone is represented by a circle whose area is proportional to its size

pack_sample <- function(df_sample) {

  packing <- circleProgressiveLayout(   df_sample$n_cells,  sizetype = "area"  )

  df_layout <- cbind(df_sample, packing)

    # Generate polygon coordinates for drawing clone outlines
  vertices <- circleLayoutVertices(   packing,  npoints = 80  )

  vertices$Barcode <- df_sample$Barcode[vertices$id]
  vertices$SampleBio <- unique(df_sample$SampleBio)

  list(
    centers = df_layout,   # clone centers and radius
    clones = vertices      # clone outlines
  )
}


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Nested circle packing function
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# Create a nested layout where:
#   - each clone is represented as a large circle
#   - individual cells are packed inside their corresponding clone

pack_one_sample_nested_MP <- function(df_sample, df_cells_sample) {
  
  # ---- Clone-level packing ----
  packing_clones <- circleProgressiveLayout(
    df_sample$n_cells,  sizetype = "area"  )
  
  df_layout <- cbind(df_sample, packing_clones)
  
  # Generate polygon coordinates for drawing clone outlines
  vertices_clones <- circleLayoutVertices(  packing_clones,    npoints = 80  )
  
  vertices_clones$Barcode <- df_sample$Barcode[vertices_clones$id]
  vertices_clones$SampleBio <- unique(df_sample$SampleBio)
  
  
  # ---- Cell-level packing ----  
  all_cells_centers <- list()
  
  for(i in seq_len(nrow(df_layout))) {
    
    clone_info <- df_layout[i, ]
    
    # Extract cells belonging to the current clone
    cells_clone <- df_cells_sample %>%
      dplyr::filter(Barcode == clone_info$Barcode)
    
     # Pack cells as equally sized circles
    packing_cells <- circleProgressiveLayout(
      rep(1, nrow(cells_clone)),
      sizetype = "area"
    )
    
     # Rescale cell packing to fit within the clone circle
    scale_factor <- clone_info$radius /
      max(  sqrt(packing_cells$x^2 + packing_cells$y^2) +    packing_cells$radius   )
    
    # Translate and scale cell coordinates to clone position
    packing_cells$x <-  packing_cells$x * scale_factor +  clone_info$x
    packing_cells$y <-  packing_cells$y * scale_factor +  clone_info$y
    packing_cells$radius <-   packing_cells$radius * scale_factor
    
    # Store cell coordinates together with metadata
    cell_centers <- cbind(  packing_cells,  cells_clone   )
    cell_centers$Barcode <- clone_info$Barcode
    cell_centers$SampleBio <- clone_info$SampleBio
    
    all_cells_centers[[i]] <- cell_centers
  }
  
  list(
    clones = vertices_clones,              # clone outlines
    cells = bind_rows(all_cells_centers),  # cell positions
    centers = df_layout                    # clone centers
  )
}


#-------------------------------------------------------------
# Nested clone / cell packing
#-------------------------------------------------------------
# Creates a nested layout where clones are represented as large
# circles containing equally sized circles corresponding to cells.
#
# color_var : metadata column copied to each cell polygon and
#             used afterwards for plotting.
pack_one_sample_nested <- function(df_sample,
                                   df_cells_sample,
                                   color_var) {

  ## Clone layout
  packing_clones <- circleProgressiveLayout(
    df_sample$n_cells,
    sizetype = "area"
  )

  df_layout <- cbind(df_sample, packing_clones)

  vertices_clones <- circleLayoutVertices(
    packing_clones,
    npoints = 50
  )

  vertices_clones$Barcode  <- df_sample$Barcode[vertices_clones$id]
  vertices_clones$SampleBio <- unique(df_sample$SampleBio)

  ## Cell layout
  all_cells_layout <- list()

  for(i in seq_len(nrow(df_layout))) {

    clone_info <- df_layout[i, ]

    cells_clone <- df_cells_sample %>%
      filter(Barcode == clone_info$Barcode)

    packing_cells <- circleProgressiveLayout(
      rep(1, nrow(cells_clone)),
      sizetype = "area"
    )

    scale_factor <-
      clone_info$radius /
      max(
        sqrt(packing_cells$x^2 + packing_cells$y^2) +
          packing_cells$radius
      )

    packing_cells$x <- packing_cells$x * scale_factor + clone_info$x
    packing_cells$y <- packing_cells$y * scale_factor + clone_info$y
    packing_cells$radius <- packing_cells$radius * scale_factor

    vertices_cells <- circleLayoutVertices(
      packing_cells,
      npoints = 20
    )

    vertices_cells$ColorValue <- cells_clone[[color_var]][vertices_cells$id]
    vertices_cells$Barcode <- clone_info$Barcode
    vertices_cells$SampleBio <- clone_info$SampleBio

    all_cells_layout[[i]] <- vertices_cells
  }

  list(
    clones = vertices_clones,
    cells = bind_rows(all_cells_layout),
    centers = df_layout
  )
}

```


### Fig 3f. Bubble Expanded

```{r}
df_cells <- anno_FP %>%
  filter(!is.na(Barcode)) %>%
  mutate(cell_id = row_number())

df_clones <- df_cells %>%
  dplyr::count(SampleBio, Barcode, 
        name = "n_cells")


# ------------------------------------------------------------
#  Generate layouts for all biological samples
# ------------------------------------------------------------

packed <- df_clones %>%
  group_split(SampleBio) %>%
  lapply(pack_sample)

# Merge layouts across samples
df_clones_vertices <- bind_rows(lapply(packed, `[[`, "clones"))
df_clones_centers  <- bind_rows(lapply(packed, `[[`, "centers"))


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# add annotation
# ------------------------------------------------------------

#Add clone expansion status
df_clones_vertices <- df_clones_vertices %>%
  left_join(anno_FP[, c("Barcode", "ExpandedClone")] %>%distinct(), by = "Barcode")%>%
  distinct()

#N cell 
df_clone_counts <- anno_FP %>%
  dplyr::count(Barcode, SampleBio, name = "n_cells")

#compute annotation point
df_clone_centroids <- df_clones_vertices %>%
  ungroup() %>%group_by(Barcode, SampleBio) %>%
  summarise(
    x = mean(x),
    y = mean(y),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(df_clone_counts, by = c("Barcode", "SampleBio"))


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Plot clone-level functional program composition
# ------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot() +
  geom_polygon( data = df_clones_vertices,
    aes(x, y,  fill = ExpandedClone, group = interaction(Barcode, SampleBio)),
    colour = "black", size = 0.4  ) +
  geom_text(data = df_clone_centroids,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = n_cells),
    size = 3,    color = "black"  ) +
  
  facet_wrap(~ SampleBio) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#d0e0ea","#4384ac", "#0a334c") %>%rev)+
  coord_equal() +
  theme_void()
```


### Fig 4d. Nested bubble Pseudotime
```{r}
# -----------------------------
# 1. Data preparation
# -----------------------------

# Keep only cells with a valid lineage barcode
df_cells <- anno_FP %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(Barcode)) %>%
  mutate(cell_id = row_number())

# Compute clone sizes (number of cells per barcode)
df_clones <- df_cells %>%
  count(SampleBio, Barcode, name = "n_cells")


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Generate nested layouts for all biological samples
# ------------------------------------------------------------

packed <- df_clones %>%
  group_split(SampleBio) %>%
  lapply(function(df_sample) {
    
    sample_name <- unique(df_sample$SampleBio)
    
     # Keep cells from the current sample only
    df_cells_sample <- df_cells %>%
      dplyr::filter(SampleBio == sample_name)
    
    # Compute nested layout
    pack_one_sample_nested( df_sample,  df_cells_sample, "pseudotime"  )
  })

# Combine layouts across samples
df_clones_vertices <- bind_rows(lapply(packed, `[[`, "clones"))
df_cells_vertices <- bind_rows(lapply(packed, `[[`, "cells"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
#  Plot nested clone architecture
# ------------------------------------------------------------
ggplot() +
  
   # Draw clone boundaries
  geom_polygon(
    data = df_clones_vertices,
    aes(x, y,   group = interaction(  Barcode,  SampleBio  )),
    fill = NA,  colour = "black",  linewidth = 0.3  ) +
  
  # Draw each cell colored by pPT
  geom_polygon(  data = df_cells_vertices,
    aes(x, y, group = interaction(id, Barcode, SampleBio),
        fill = ColorValue),
    colour = NA  ) + 
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma") +
  
  facet_wrap(    ~ SampleBio  ) +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_void()
```
### Fig 5b. Nested Cell Fate
```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Generate nested layouts for all biological samples
# ------------------------------------------------------------

packed <- df_clones %>%
  group_split(SampleBio) %>%
  lapply(function(df_sample) {
    
    sample_name <- unique(df_sample$SampleBio)
    
     # Keep cells from the current sample only
    df_cells_sample <- df_cells %>%
      dplyr::filter(SampleBio == sample_name)
    
    # Compute nested layout
    pack_one_sample_nested( df_sample,  df_cells_sample, "annot_rna_joint_alv"  )
  })

# Combine layouts across samples
df_clones_vertices <- bind_rows(lapply(packed, `[[`, "clones"))
df_cells_vertices <- bind_rows(lapply(packed, `[[`, "cells"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
#  Plot nested clone architecture
# ------------------------------------------------------------
ggplot() +
  
   # Draw clone boundaries
  geom_polygon(
    data = df_clones_vertices,
    aes(x, y,   group = interaction(  Barcode,  SampleBio  )),
    fill = NA,  colour = "black",  linewidth = 0.3  ) +
  
  # Draw each cell colored by pPT
  geom_polygon(  data = df_cells_vertices,
    aes(x, y, group = interaction(id, Barcode, SampleBio),
        fill = ColorValue),
    colour = NA  ) + 
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols_fate) +
  facet_wrap(    ~ SampleBio  ) +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_void()
```




### Fig 4a. Nested Bubbles - clonal architecture and functional programs

```{r}
# -----------------------------
# 1. Data preparation
# -----------------------------
anno_FP <-  read.delim(paste0(Input_Dir, "/FunctionalProgramAssignment.txt"))


# Identify functional program (metaprogram) columns
mp_cols <- grep("^Bin_MP", colnames(anno_FP), value = TRUE)

# Keep only cells with a valid lineage barcode
df_cells <- anno_FP %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(Barcode)) %>%
  mutate(cell_id = row_number())

# Compute clone sizes (number of cells per barcode)
df_clones <- df_cells %>%
  count(SampleBio, Barcode, name = "n_cells")




# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Generate nested layouts for all biological samples
# ------------------------------------------------------------

packed <- df_clones %>%
  group_split(SampleBio) %>%
  lapply(function(df_sample) {
    
    sample_name <- unique(df_sample$SampleBio)
    
     # Keep cells from the current sample only
    df_cells_sample <- df_cells %>%
      dplyr::filter(SampleBio == sample_name)
    
    # Compute nested layout
    pack_one_sample_nested_MP( df_sample,  df_cells_sample  )
  })

# Combine layouts across samples
df_clones_vertices <- bind_rows(lapply(packed, `[[`, "clones"))
df_cells_centers <- bind_rows(lapply(packed, `[[`, "cells"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Prepare functional program composition for pie charts
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# Convert logical functional program assignments (TRUE/FALSE)
# into numeric values (1/0) required by geom_scatterpie()
df_cells_pies <- df_cells_centers %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(mp_cols),~ as.numeric(.x)  ))

# Add an additional category for cells without any active program
df_cells_pies <- df_cells_pies %>%
  mutate(
    n_MP = rowSums(across(all_of(mp_cols)), na.rm = TRUE),
    No_MP = as.numeric(n_MP == 0)  )

# Functional program columns used for pie chart composition
cols_use <- mp_cols


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Plot nested clone architecture
# ------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot() +
  
   # Draw clone boundaries
  geom_polygon(
    data = df_clones_vertices,
    aes(x, y,   group = interaction(  Barcode,  SampleBio  )),
    fill = NA,  colour = "black",  linewidth = 0.3  ) +
  
  # Draw each cell as a pie chart representing its active functional programs
  geom_scatterpie(
  data = df_cells_pies,
  aes(x = x, y = y, r = radius),
  cols = c("No_MP", cols_use),  color = "grey40") +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = Bin_colors)+
  facet_wrap(    ~ SampleBio  ) +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_void()

```

### Fig 3f. Bubble & Pies - Functional program composition of individual clones

```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Compute functional program composition at the clone level
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# For each clone, calculate the proportion of cells assigned to each functional program
df_clones_MP <- df_cells %>%
  group_by(SampleBio, Barcode) %>%
  summarise(
    n_cells = n(),
    across(all_of(mp_cols), ~ mean(as.numeric(.x), na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )



# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Generate layouts for all biological samples
# ------------------------------------------------------------

packed <- df_clones_MP %>%
  group_split(SampleBio) %>%
  lapply(pack_sample)

# Merge layouts across samples
df_clones_vertices <- bind_rows(lapply(packed, `[[`, "clones"))
df_clones_centers  <- bind_rows(lapply(packed, `[[`, "centers")) %>%
  mutate(
    n_MP = rowSums(across(all_of(mp_cols)), na.rm = TRUE),
     # Add info for clones lacking any detected functional program
    No_MP = as.numeric(n_MP == 0)
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Plot clone-level functional program composition
# ------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot() +

  # contours clones
  geom_polygon(
    data = df_clones_vertices,
    aes(x, y, group = interaction(Barcode, SampleBio)),
    fill = NA,  colour = "black",  linewidth = 0.3  ) +

  # pies = MP composition du clone
  geom_scatterpie(data = df_clones_centers,
    aes(x = x, y = y, r = radius),
    cols = c("No_MP", mp_cols),   color = "grey40"  ) +

  facet_wrap(~ SampleBio) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = Bin_colors)+
  coord_equal() +
  theme_void()
```

```{r}
df_clones_centers %>% 
  filter(SampleBio %in% c("8964_G4", "8415_G2-3", "8718_D2-3"),
                           !is.na(Barcode),
                           !No_MP != 0) %>%
  mutate( NombreMP = rowSums(across(all_of(mp_cols)) > 0, 
                             na.rm = TRUE) ,
          x_jitter = (n_cells + runif(n(), -0.05, 0.05) * n_cells ),
          y_jitter = NombreMP + runif(n(), -0.05, 0.05),
          radius_scaled = radius * 0.4) %>%
  
  ggplot( ) +
  geom_smooth(aes(x = n_cells, y = NombreMP), 
              method = "loess", se = F, color = "grey")+
  geom_scatterpie(  aes(x = x_jitter   , y = y_jitter , r = radius_scaled),
                    cols = c("No_MP", mp_cols),   color = "black" , alpha = 1 ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = Bin_colors)+
  coord_equal()+
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0,50))+
  theme_classic()
```



```{r}
# RĂ©sumĂ© au niveau des clones
plot_df <- df_clones_MP %>%
  mutate(
    n_MP = rowSums(across(all_of(mp_cols)) > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    df_cells %>%
      group_by(SampleBio, Barcode) %>%
      summarise(CloneSize = n(), .groups = "drop"),
    by = c("SampleBio", "Barcode")
  )

library(dplyr)
library(scatterpie)

plot_df <- df_clones_MP %>%
  left_join(
    df_cells %>%
      count(SampleBio, Barcode, name = "CloneSize"),
    by = c("SampleBio", "Barcode")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    n_MP = rowSums(across(all_of(mp_cols), ~ .x > 0))
  )

pie_df <- plot_df %>%
  mutate(
    radius = sqrt(CloneSize) / 10   # Ă  ajuster
  )

ggplot() +
  geom_scatterpie(
    data = pie_df,
    aes(x = CloneSize, y = n_MP),
    cols = mp_cols,
    color = "grey40"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = Bin_colors) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = "Clone size",
    y = "Number of functional programs"
  )
```


## Fig 3g. Boxplots - Intraclonal heterogeneity
```{r}
# Load RNA distance to clonal centroid table

df_dist <- read.delim(paste0(Input_Dir, "/RNADistCentroid.txt"))

# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Statistics
# --------------------------------------------------------------
df <- df_dist %>%
  subset( is.na(Clone)==F)

tuk <- df %>%
  tukey_hsd(dist_to_centroid ~ Group) %>%
  add_xy_position(x = "Cluster")


# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Visualize transcriptional heterogeneity across Group (biological Age)
# --------------------------------------------------------------
ggplot(subset(df_dist, is.na(Clone)==F),
       aes(x = Group, y = dist_to_centroid)) +
  geom_boxplot(outliers = F, aes( fill = Group)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "anova",
                     label.y = max(df$dist_to_centroid, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.5) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(tuk, label = "p.adj.signif", step.increase = 0.1,  
                     xmin = "group1",xmax = "group2") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#C0D69F", "#61980E"))+
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  labs(y = "Distance to clone-centroid - RNA", x = "") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```
```{r}
# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Statistics
# --------------------------------------------------------------
df <- df_dist %>%
  subset( is.na(Expand)==F)

tuk <- df %>%
  tukey_hsd(dist_to_centroid ~ Expand) %>%
  add_xy_position(x = "Cluster")


# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Visualize transcriptional heterogeneity across clone classes
# --------------------------------------------------------------
ggplot(subset(df_dist, is.na(Expand)==F),
       aes(x = Expand, y = dist_to_centroid)) +
  geom_boxplot(outliers = F, aes( fill = Expand)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "anova",
                     label.y = max(df$dist_to_centroid, na.rm = TRUE) ) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(tuk, label = "p.adj.signif", step.increase = 0.1,  
                     xmin = "group1",xmax = "group2") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#d0e0ea","#4384ac", "#0a334c") %>%rev)+
  scale_x_discrete(limits = c("Small", "Int", "Expanded"))+
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  labs(y = "Distance to clone-centroid - RNA", x = "") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```


```{r}
# Load EpiG H3K4me1  distance to clonal centroid table
df_dist <- read.delim(paste0(Input_Dir, "/H3K4me1DistCentroid.txt"))

# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Statistics
# --------------------------------------------------------------
df <- df_dist %>%
  subset( is.na(Clone)==F)

tuk <- df %>%
  tukey_hsd(dist_to_centroid ~ Group) %>%
  add_xy_position(x = "Group")


# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Visualize transcriptional heterogeneity across Group (biological Age)
# --------------------------------------------------------------
ggplot(subset(df_dist, is.na(Clone)==F),
       aes(x = Group, y = dist_to_centroid)) +
  geom_boxplot(outliers = F, aes( fill = Group)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "anova",
                     label.y = 2) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(tuk, label = "p.adj.signif", step.increase = 0.1,  
                     xmin = "group1",xmax = "group2") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#C0D69F", "#61980E"))+
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  labs(y = "Distance to clone-centroid", x = "") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```
```{r}
# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Statistics
# --------------------------------------------------------------
df <- df_dist %>%
  subset( is.na(ExpandedClone)==F)

tuk <- df %>%
  tukey_hsd(dist_to_centroid ~ ExpandedClone) %>%
  add_xy_position(x = "ExpandedClone")


# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Visualize transcriptional heterogeneity across clone classes
# --------------------------------------------------------------
ggplot(subset(df_dist, is.na(ExpandedClone)==F),
       aes(x = ExpandedClone, y = dist_to_centroid)) +
  geom_boxplot(outliers = F, aes( fill = ExpandedClone)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "anova",
                     label.y = max(df$dist_to_centroid, na.rm = TRUE) ) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(tuk, label = "p.adj.signif", step.increase = 0.1,  
                     xmin = "group1",xmax = "group2") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#d0e0ea","#4384ac", "#0a334c") %>%rev)+
  scale_x_discrete(limits = c("Small", "Int", "Expanded"))+
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  labs(y = "Distance to clone-centroid", x = "") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

```{r}
# Number of cells per clone category
table(df_dist$ExpandedClone)
```



```{r}
# Load EpiG H3K27me3 distance to clonal centroid table

df_dist <- read.delim(paste0(Input_Dir, "/H3K27me3DistCentroid.txt"))

# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Statistics
# --------------------------------------------------------------
df <- df_dist %>%
  subset( is.na(Clone)==F)

tuk <- df %>%
  tukey_hsd(dist_to_centroid ~ Group) %>%
  add_xy_position(x = "Group")


# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Visualize transcriptional heterogeneity across Group (biological Age)
# --------------------------------------------------------------
ggplot(subset(df_dist, is.na(Clone)==F),
       aes(x = Group, y = dist_to_centroid)) +
  geom_boxplot(outliers = F, aes( fill = Group)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "anova",
                     label.y = 2) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(tuk, label = "p.adj.signif", step.increase = 0.1,  
                     xmin = "group1",xmax = "group2") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#C0D69F", "#61980E"))+
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  labs(y = "Distance to clone-centroid", x = "") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```
```{r}
# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Statistics
# --------------------------------------------------------------
df <- df_dist %>%
  subset( is.na(ExpandedClone)==F)

tuk <- df %>%
  tukey_hsd(dist_to_centroid ~ ExpandedClone) %>%
  add_xy_position(x = "ExpandedClone")


# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Visualize transcriptional heterogeneity across clone classes
# --------------------------------------------------------------
ggplot(subset(df_dist, is.na(ExpandedClone)==F),
       aes(x = ExpandedClone, y = dist_to_centroid)) +
  geom_boxplot(outliers = F, aes( fill = ExpandedClone)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "anova",
                     label.y = max(df$dist_to_centroid, na.rm = TRUE) ) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(tuk, label = "p.adj.signif", step.increase = 0.1,  
                     xmin = "group1",xmax = "group2") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#d0e0ea","#4384ac", "#0a334c") %>%rev)+
  scale_x_discrete(limits = c("Small", "Int", "Expanded"))+
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  labs(y = "Distance to clone-centroid", x = "") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

```{r}
# Number of cells per clone category
table(df_dist$ExpandedClone)
```

## Fig 3h. Boxplots - FP by clone

```{r}
# Load Number of FP for each clone table

clone_stats <- read.delim(paste0(Input_Dir, "/FP_byClone.txt"))
# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Statistics
# --------------------------------------------------------------
df <- subset(clone_stats, is.na(ExpandedClone)==F)
tuk <- df %>%
  tukey_hsd(n_active_MP ~ ExpandedClone) %>%
  add_xy_position(x = "ExpandedClone")


# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Visualize transcriptional heterogeneity across clone classes
# --------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot(clone_stats,
       aes(x = ExpandedClone, n_active_MP)) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = ExpandedClone))+
  geom_point(aes(fill = ExpandedClone),
              alpha = 0.8, color = "black", shape = 21, size = 3,
             position = position_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0.02)) +

  stat_compare_means(method = "anova",
                     label.y = max(df$dist_to_centroid, na.rm = TRUE) ) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(tuk, label = "p.adj.signif", step.increase = 0.1,  
                     xmin = "group1",xmax = "group2") +
  
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#d0e0ea","#4384ac", "#0a334c") %>%rev)+
  scale_x_discrete(limits = c("Small", "Int", "Expanded"))+
  labs(   x = "Clone size",   y = "N MP per clone"  )
  


```


```{r}
ggplot(subset(clone_stats, is.na(ExpandedClone)==F),
       aes(clone_size, MP_entropy)) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = ExpandedClone), 
             alpha = 0.8, color = "black", shape = 21, size = 3,
             position = position_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0.02)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", se = F) +
  stat_cor(   method = "pearson",   
              aes(label = paste(after_stat(r.label),after_stat(p.label),  
                                sep = "~`,`~")),
    label.x.npc = 0.8,   label.y.npc = 1.0,   size = 2  ) +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("#d0e0ea","#4384ac", "#0a334c") %>%rev)+
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  labs(   x = "Clone size",   y = "MP entropy per clone"  ) 
```

## Fig 3j.  Heatmap - Clonal expansion and FP
```{r}
# Load Number of FP for each clone table
clone_stats <- read.delim(paste0(Input_Dir, "/FP_byClone.txt"))

# Load functional program assignment table
df <-  read.delim(paste0(Input_Dir, "/FunctionalProgramAssignment.txt"))

# Identify columns corresponding to FP assignments
mp_cols <- grep("^Bin_MP", colnames(df), value = TRUE)

# Compute number of FP active per cell
df <- df %>%
  mutate( n_MP = rowSums(across(all_of(mp_cols)),
                   na.rm = TRUE)  )


# restrict df to monogland
df_sub <- df %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    SampleBio %in% c("8964_G4", "8718_D2-3", "8415_G2-3"),
    !is.na(Barcode)
  )

clone_stats <- clone_stats %>%
  mutate(
    clone_group = case_when(
      clone_size <= 3 & clone_size >=1  ~ "small",
      clone_size <= 7 & clone_size > 3 ~ "medium",
      TRUE ~ "expanded"    )  )
table(clone_stats$clone_group)

plot_df <- df_sub %>%
   dplyr::select(n_MP,mp_cols, Barcode ) %>%
   dplyr::filter(n_MP > 0) %>%
  mutate(clone_group = clone_stats$clone_group[    match(Barcode, clone_stats$Barcode)  ]) %>%
  pivot_longer(    all_of(mp_cols),    names_to = "MP",    values_to = "active"  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(active == 1) %>% ungroup() %>%
  group_by(clone_group, MP) %>%
  summarise(    n = n(),    .groups = "drop"  )

totals <- df_sub %>%
  mutate(
    clone_group = clone_stats$clone_group[
      match(Barcode, clone_stats$Barcode)
    ]
  ) %>%
  group_by(clone_group) %>%
  summarise(
    n_cells = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )


plot_df <- plot_df %>%
  left_join(totals, by = "clone_group") %>%
  mutate(
    frac = n / n_cells * 100
  )


ggplot(plot_df,
       aes(MP, clone_group, fill = frac)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "Spectral", direction = -1) +

  scale_x_discrete(limits = paste0("Bin_MP", c(10, 12, 16, " ", 
                                               14, 7, 15, ".",
                                               3, 4, 9) %>% rev))+
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)  ) +  
  scale_y_discrete(limits  = c( "small", "medium", "expanded") )+
  labs(    x = NULL,    y = NULL,    fill = "% cells\nMP+"  )+
  coord_flip()
```
## Fig 3l. Heatmap â€“ Differentially Expressed Genes (DEGs) in expanding clones
```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Load input matrices
# ------------------------------------------------------------
mat_deg <- read.csv(paste0(Input_Dir,  "/mat_degSup5.csv"))
DEGs_sup5 <- read.csv(paste0(Input_Dir,  "/DEGs_sup5.csv"))

mat_multi <- read.csv(paste0(Input_Dir, "/mat_degSup5_UP_multi.csv"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Build clone annotation table
# ------------------------------------------------------------
annot_col <- DEGs_sup5 %>%
  dplyr::select(clone, sampleOrigine, clonesize) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  as.data.frame()
rownames(annot_col) <- annot_col$clone

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Format binary gene Ă— clone matrices
# ------------------------------------------------------------
mat <- as.matrix(mat_deg[, -c(1,2)]) # remove gene metadata columns
rownames(mat) <- mat_deg$gene

rownames(mat_multi) <- mat_multi$gene
mat_multi <- as.matrix(mat_multi[, -c(1,2)])

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Color mapping for binary DEG matrix
# ------------------------------------------------------------
col_fun <- c("0" = "white", "1" = "#3a5b6f")


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Format annotation metadata
# ------------------------------------------------------------
annot_col <- annot_col %>%
  mutate(
    sampleOrigine = as.character(sampleOrigine),
    clonesize = as.numeric(clonesize)
  )

# Predefined palette for sample origin
sample_colors <- cols_MonoGland

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Column annotation (clone metadata)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
ha_col <- HeatmapAnnotation(
  df = annot_col[, c("sampleOrigine", "clonesize")],
  col = list(
    sampleOrigine = sample_colors,
    clonesize = colorRamp2(
      range(annot_col$clonesize, na.rm = TRUE),
      c("lightgrey", "black")
    )
  ),
  annotation_legend_param = list(
    sampleOrigine = list(title = "Sample origin"),
    clonesize = list(title = "Clone size")
  )
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Row annotation: Marker frequency
# ------------------------------------------------------------
row_sum <- rowSums(mat)
ha_bar <- rowAnnotation(
  "Clone count" = anno_barplot(
    row_sum,
    bar_width = 1,
    border = FALSE,
    gp = gpar(fill = "#3a5b6f"),
    width = unit(2, "cm")
  )
)



# ============================================================
# HEATMAP 1: all DEGs
# ============================================================
m1 <- Heatmap(
  mat,
  name = "DEG",
  col = col_fun,
  show_row_names = FALSE,
  show_column_names = FALSE,
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_columns = TRUE,
  clustering_distance_rows = "maximum",
  clustering_method_rows = "ward.D2",
  row_title = "Genes",
  column_title = "Clones",
  top_annotation = ha_col,
  heatmap_legend_param = list(
    at = c(0, 1),
    labels = c("Absent", "Present")
  )
)+ ha_bar

draw(m1, heatmap_legend_side = "bottom", annotation_legend_side = "right")
```

Extended Fig 6c
```{r}
# ============================================================
# HEATMAP 2: subset (frequent DEGs)
# ============================================================

row_sum <- rowSums(mat_multi)

ha_bar <- rowAnnotation(
  "Clone count" = anno_barplot(
    row_sum,
    bar_width = 1,
    border = FALSE,
    gp = gpar(fill = "#3a5b6f"),
    width = unit(2, "cm")
  )
)



m2 <- Heatmap(
  mat_multi,
  name = "DEG",
  col = col_fun,
  show_row_names = TRUE,
  row_names_side = "left",
  show_column_names = FALSE,
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_columns = TRUE,
  clustering_distance_rows = "maximum",
  clustering_method_rows = "ward.D2",
  row_title = "Genes",
  column_title = "Clones",
  top_annotation = ha_col,
  heatmap_legend_param = list(
    at = c(0, 1),
    labels = c("Absent", "Present")
  ),
  row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 6, fontface = "plain"), 
)+  ha_bar


draw(m2, heatmap_legend_side = "bottom", annotation_legend_side = "right")
```
Extended Fig 6a

Number of DEGs vs. Clone size per sample

```{r}
n_DEGs <- DEGs_sup5%>%
  group_by(clone, clonesize, sampleOrigine) %>%
  summarise(n_DEGs = n_distinct(gene), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(n_DEGs, aes(x =  clonesize, y = n_DEGs, fill = sampleOrigine)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8, position = position_jitter(width = 0.1, height = 0), 
             shape = 21, color = "black", size = 3)  +

  scale_fill_manual(values = rev(c( "#b6cf53" ,"#77b836", "#416c2c")))+
  scale_x_continuous(limits  = c(0, 25))+
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank() ) +
  labs( x = "Clone size",
    y = "Number of DEGs",
    size = "Clone size",
    title = "Number of DEGs per clone across samples" )
```

## Fig 5d. Violin - Cell Fate switch 
```{r}
# Load cell Fate switch score table
CellFateSwitch <- read.delim(paste0(Input_Dir, "/CellFateSwitch.txt"))

# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Statistics
# --------------------------------------------------------------
stat <-  CellFateSwitch %>%
  t_test(switch_score ~ Group) %>%
  add_xy_position(x = "Group")


# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Visualize transcriptional heterogeneity across Group (biological Age)
# --------------------------------------------------------------
ggplot(CellFateSwitch,
       aes(x = Group, y = switch_score)) +
 
  geom_violin(outliers = F, aes( fill = Group)) +
  geom_point( aes( fill = Group), alpha = 0.5, shape = 21,
              position= position_jitter(width = 0.1, height = 0))+
  
  stat_summary( geom = "point", fun.y = "mean",   
                col = "black",  size = 3,  shape = 21,  fill = "black"  )+
  stat_pvalue_manual(stat, label = "p", step.increase = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#C0D69F", "#61980E"))+
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  labs(y = "Distance to clone-centroid - RNA", x = "") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```




##Fig 5e. Barplot +GAM - temporal progression FP acquisition



```{r}
# Load functional program assignment table
df <-  read.delim(paste0(Input_Dir, "/FunctionalProgramAssignment.txt"))

# Identify columns corresponding to FP assignments
mp_cols <- grep("^Bin_MP", colnames(df), value = TRUE)

# Compute number of FP active per cell
df <- df %>%
  mutate( n_MP = rowSums(across(all_of(mp_cols)),
                   na.rm = TRUE)  )

# -----------------------------
# Prepare data for plotting
# -----------------------------
df_plot <- df %>%
  #Remove control cells
  dplyr::filter(!Group %in% c("Rosa", "CreN") ) %>% 

  # Bin cells along pseudotime into deciles (10 equal groups)
  mutate(   pt_bin = ntile(pseudotime, 10)  )

# Keep only pseudotime bins with sufficient cell numbers (>= 50 cells)
valid_bins <- df_plot %>%
  count(pt_bin) %>%
  dplyr::filter(n >= 50) %>%
  pull(pt_bin)

# Restrict analysis to valid bins and categorize MP usage per cell
plot_df <- df_plot %>%
  dplyr::filter(pt_bin %in% valid_bins) %>%
  mutate(n_MP_group = case_when(n_MP %in% c(0) ~ "0",
                                n_MP %in% c(1) ~ "1",
                                n_MP %in% c(2, 3) ~ "2-3",
                                TRUE ~ ">=4")) %>%
  mutate(n_MP_group = factor(n_MP_group, levels = c("0", "1", "2-3", ">=4"))) %>%
  count(pt_bin, n_MP_group) %>%
  group_by(pt_bin) %>%
  mutate(   # Normalize within each pseudotime bin (fraction of cells per category)
    frac = n / sum(n)
  )

# -----------------------------
# Visualization
# -----------------------------


ggplot(plot_df,
       aes(x = pt_bin,   y = frac, fill = factor(n_MP_group))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity",    color = "black",    linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#eb6983", "#fbe1e6", "#dfd1d3", "#f6f6f6") %>% rev) +
  theme(  axis.text.x = element_text(      angle = 45,  hjust = 1  ),   
        legend.position = "right"  )+  
  theme_classic() +
  labs(fill = "# MPs")
```

```{r}
df_test <- df_plot %>%
  filter(pt_bin %in% c(1, 10)) %>%
  mutate(
    n_MP_group = case_when(
      n_MP %in% c(0, 1) ~ "0-1",
      n_MP >= 2         ~ ">=2"
    )
  )

tab <- table(df_test$pt_bin, df_test$n_MP_group)

tab
chisq.test(tab)
```


```{r}
library(mgcv)
# -----------------------------------------
# GAM analysis: does number od FPs change with pseudotime?
# -----------------------------------------

fit <- gam(n_MP ~ s(pseudotime),
           family = poisson(),
           data = df %>% dplyr::filter(!Group %in% c("Rosa", "CreN") ) ) 

summary(fit)

# Visualize fitted smooth relationship
plot(fit)
```

## Fig 6e - test stat
```{r}
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Filter metadata and keep only selected conditions
# ---------------------------------------------------------
data_plot <- seurat@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::filter(condition %in% c("CreP_EARLY",  "CreP_LATE", "CreN"))
  
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# stats
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# KS test basal
p_basal <- ks.test(
  data_plot$DPT_K27_BLP_from_lp[
    data_plot$condition %in% c("CreP_LATE", "CreP_EARLY") &
      data_plot$annot_k27_joint == "basal"
  ],
  data_plot$DPT_K27_BLP_from_lp[
    data_plot$condition %in% c("CreN", "Rosa") &
      data_plot$annot_k27_joint == "basal"
  ]
)$p.value

# KS test LP
p_lp <- ks.test(
  data_plot$DPT_K27_BLP_from_lp[
    data_plot$condition %in% c("CreP_LATE", "CreP_EARLY") &
      data_plot$annot_k27_joint == "LP"
  ],
  data_plot$DPT_K27_BLP_from_lp[
    data_plot$condition %in% c("CreN", "Rosa") &
      data_plot$annot_k27_joint == "LP"
  ]
)$p.value

# Adjust p-values
padj <- p.adjust(c(p_basal, p_lp), method = "BH")
names(padj) <- c("basal", "LP")

padj

```
```{r}
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# stats
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# KS test basal
p_basal <- ks.test(
  data_plot$DPT_K4_BLP_from_lp_int[
    data_plot$condition %in% c("CreP_LATE", "CreP_EARLY") &
      data_plot$annot_k4_joint == "basal"
  ],
  data_plot$DPT_K4_BLP_from_lp_int[
    data_plot$condition %in% c("CreN", "Rosa") &
      data_plot$annot_k4_joint == "basal"
  ]
)$p.value

# KS test LP
p_lp <- ks.test(
  data_plot$DPT_K4_BLP_from_lp_int[
    data_plot$condition %in% c("CreP_LATE", "CreP_EARLY") &
      data_plot$annot_k4_joint == "LP"
  ],
  data_plot$DPT_K4_BLP_from_lp_int[
    data_plot$condition %in% c("CreN", "Rosa") &
      data_plot$annot_k4_joint == "LP"
  ]
)$p.value

# Adjust p-values
padj <- p.adjust(c(p_basal, p_lp), method = "BH")
names(padj) <- c("basal", "LP")

padj

```




## Fig 6h. Density - Pseudotime vs. cell fate

```{r}
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Filter metadata and keep only selected conditions
# ---------------------------------------------------------
data_plot <- seurat_rna@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::filter(condition %in% c("CreP_EARLY",  "CreP_LATE", "CreN"))
  
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Keep only groups (annot_rna_joint_alv Ă— condition)
# with more than 50 cells
# ---------------------------------------------------------

keep_groups <- data_plot %>%
  count(condition, annot_rna_joint_alv) %>%
  dplyr::filter(n > 50) %>%
  dplyr::select(condition, annot_rna_joint_alv)

data_plot <- data_plot %>%
  inner_join(keep_groups, by = c("condition", "annot_rna_joint_alv"))


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Compute summary statistics per group
# ---------------------------------------------------------
stats <- data_plot %>%
  group_by(annot_rna_joint_alv, condition) %>%
  summarise(
    mean = mean(pseudotime, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd   = sd(pseudotime, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

# Create vertical offsets to avoid overlap
stats <- stats %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  mutate(
    y_pos = 1.02 + as.numeric(factor(annot_rna_joint_alv)) * 0.1
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Plot pseudotime distributions per cell state and condition
# ---------------------------------------------------------
ggplot(data_plot,
       aes(x = pseudotime, fill = annot_rna_joint_alv, colour = annot_rna_joint_alv)) +
  geom_density( alpha = 0.6,    linewidth = 0.5,    adjust = 1.2  )+
  geom_jitter(  aes(y = -0.1),   
              height = 0.05,  width = 0,alpha = 0.3,  size = 0.7, show.legend = FALSE  ) +
  geom_segment( data = stats,
    aes(x = mean - sd,  xend = mean + sd, y = y_pos,yend = y_pos),  linewidth = 2 ) +
  geom_point(  data = stats,
               aes(x = mean, y = y_pos),   size = 3 ) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols_fate)+
  scale_color_manual(values = cols_fate)+
  labs(    x = "Pseudotime",    y = NULL,    fill = NULL,    colour = NULL  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 18) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    axis.line.y = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank()  )+
  facet_grid(condition ~.)
```

```{r}
ks.test(
  data_plot$pseudotime[data_plot$condition == "CreP_LATE" &
                         data_plot$annot_rna_joint_alv == "basal"],
  data_plot$pseudotime[data_plot$condition == "CreP_LATE"&
                         data_plot$annot_rna_joint_alv == "LP"]
)
```

## Fig 6i. Violin -  FP by Fate

```{r}
# Load functional program assignment table
df <-  read.delim(paste0(Input_Dir, "/FunctionalProgramAssignment.txt"))

# Identify columns corresponding to FP assignments
mp_cols <- grep("^Bin_MP", colnames(df), value = TRUE)

# Compute number of FP active per cell
df <- df %>%
  mutate( n_MP = rowSums(across(all_of(mp_cols)),
                   na.rm = TRUE)  )
# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Statistics
# --------------------------------------------------------------
dat <- df %>%
    filter( !Group %in% c("Rosa","CreN") ,
             annot_rna_joint_alv %in% c(  "LP","Alv",  "basal"  ) )
tuk <- dat %>%
  dunn_test(n_MP ~ annot_rna_joint_alv, p.adjust.method = "BH") %>%
  add_xy_position(x = "annot_rna_joint_alv")


# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Visualize Number of active FPs per cell across cell fate status
# --------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot(  df %>%filter( !Group %in% c("Rosa","CreN") ),
  aes(    annot_rna_joint_alv,    n_MP )) +
  geom_violin(aes(  fill = annot_rna_joint_alv ),    trim = FALSE  ) +
  geom_boxplot(  aes(  fill = annot_rna_joint_alv ),  width = 0.12, outliers = F  ) +
  
  stat_compare_means(method = "kruskal",
                     label.y = max(dat$n_MP, na.rm = TRUE) ) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(tuk, label = "p.adj.signif", step.increase = 0.1,  
                     xmin = "group1",xmax = "group2") +
  
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = c( "LP", "Alv", "basal") )+
  scale_fill_manual(values =cols_fate )+
  labs(    x = NULL,    y = "Number of active FPs per cell"  )+
  guides(fill="none")
```


```{r}
# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Shannon entropy : Number of active FPs per cell across cell fate status
# --------------------------------------------------------------
library(entropy)

score_cols <- grep("^Bin_MP", #on discrete values
                    colnames(df),  value = TRUE)

df$MP_entropy <- apply(  df[, score_cols],  1,
                         
  function(x){

    x[is.na(x)] <- 0
    x[x < 0] <- 0
    if(sum(x) == 0){
      return(0)
    }

    p <- x / sum(x)

    entropy::entropy(p)
  }
)
# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Statistics
# --------------------------------------------------------------
dat <- df %>%
    filter( !Group %in% c("Rosa","CreN") ,
             annot_rna_joint_alv %in% c(  "LP","Alv",  "basal"  ) )
tuk <- dat %>%
  tukey_hsd(MP_entropy ~ annot_rna_joint_alv) %>%
  add_xy_position(x = "annot_rna_joint_alv")

# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Visu
# --------------------------------------------------------------
ggplot(  df %>%
    filter(!Group %in% c("Rosa","CreN"),
             annot_rna_joint_alv %in% c(  "LP","Alv",  "basal"  )   ),
  aes(    annot_rna_joint_alv,    MP_entropy )) +
  geom_violin( aes(  fill = annot_rna_joint_alv ),   
               trim = FALSE,    alpha = 0.8  ) +
  geom_boxplot( aes(  fill = annot_rna_joint_alv ),   
                width = .12,    outlier.shape = NA  ) +
  stat_summary(    fun = median,    geom = "point",    size = 2  ) +
  
  stat_compare_means(method = "anova",
                     label.y = max(dat$n_MP, na.rm = TRUE) ) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(tuk, label = "p.adj.signif", step.increase = 0.1,  
                     xmin = "group1",xmax = "group2") +
  
  
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  labs(    x = NULL,    y = "MP entropy per cell"  )+
  scale_x_discrete(limits = c( "LP", "Alv", "basal") )+
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols_fate )+
  guides(fill="none")

```
## Fig 6j. Radar - FP by Fate

Note: requires running 0_Utils first

```{r}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Load functional program assignment table
# ------------------------------------------------------------
df <-  read.delim(paste0(Input_Dir, "/FunctionalProgramAssignment.txt"))

# Identify columns corresponding to FP assignments
mp_cols <- grep("^Bin_MP", colnames(df), value = TRUE)

# Compute number of FP active per cell
df <- df %>%
  mutate( n_MP = rowSums(across(all_of(mp_cols)),
                   na.rm = TRUE)  )

# -----------------------------
# Prepare data for plotting
# -----------------------------
# Reshape data: long format (cell Ă— functional program)
df_long <- df %>%
  select(SampleBio, annot_rna_joint_alv, umap1, umap2, all_of(hallmark_cols)) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(hallmark_cols),
    names_to = "MP",
    values_to = "value"  ) %>%
  filter(value == TRUE) 

# Compute functional program frequency per cell (aka UMAP coordinate)
df_pie <- df_long %>%
  distinct(SampleBio, annot_rna_joint_alv, umap1, umap2, MP) %>%
  count(umap1, umap2, MP, name = "n") %>%
  group_by(umap1, umap2) %>%
  mutate(freq = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()


# Convert to wide format (one column per functional program)
df_wide <- df_pie %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = MP,  values_from = freq,   values_fill = 0  ) %>%
  filter(  is.finite(umap1),    is.finite(umap2)  ) %>% 
    left_join(# Add biosample & Group
    df %>% select(SampleBio, annot_rna_joint_alv, Group, umap1, umap2),
    by = c("umap1", "umap2")  )

# Aggregate: mean FP activity per fate group
df_sample <- df_wide %>%
  group_by(annot_rna_joint_alv) %>%
  summarise(across(  all_of(hallmark_cols),   ~ mean(.x , na.rm = TRUE) * 100    ), #% positive cell by hallmark
    .groups = "drop"  )

# Prepare radar plot input
df_radar <- df_sample
rownames(df_radar) <- df_radar$annot_rna_joint_alv

df_radar <- df_radar[, c("annot_rna_joint_alv", hallmark_cols)]

thr <- max(df_radar[, hallmark_cols], na.rm = TRUE)


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Radar plot
# ------------------------------------------------------------
df_radar %>%
  filter(!annot_rna_joint_alv  == "HS") %>%
  ggradar_custom(
  values.radar = c("0%", paste0(round(thr/2), "%"), paste0(round(thr), "%")),
  group.line.width = 1,
  group.point.size = 3,
  background.circle.colour = "white",
   group.colours = cols_fate,
  gridline.mid.colour = "grey"
)

```


## Control for Pgen
### Ext Fig5h. Pgen - Intraclonal RNA heterogeneity
```{r}
# Load transcriptomic distance to clonal centroid ^Pgen table

res_pgen_dist <- read.delim(paste0(Input_Dir, "/RNADistCentroid_Pgen.txt"))

ggplot(subset(res_pgen_dist, is.na(ExpandedClone )==F), 
       aes(x = -log10(threshold),  y = median_dist,   colour = ExpandedClone)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  theme_classic() +
   geom_text(aes(label = n_clones),
    vjust = -1,    size = 3,    show.legend = FALSE  )+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#d0e0ea","#4384ac", "#0a334c") %>%rev)+
  labs(x = "Maximum Pgen retained (-log10)",
       y = "Mean distance to centroid")

```


### Ext fig5g. Pgen - FP by clone


Median clone poly-functionality was then visualized as a function of increasingly stringent Pgen cutoffs.
```{r}
# Load Number of FP for each clone ^ Pgen table 
res_pgen_MP <- read.delim(paste0(Input_Dir, "/FP_byClone_Pgen.txt"))


ggplot(subset(res_pgen_MP,is.na(ExpandedClone)==F),
       aes(x = -log10(threshold),   y = median_MP,   colour = ExpandedClone)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_text(aes(label = n_clones),
    vjust = -1,    size = 3,    show.legend = FALSE  )+
    geom_vline(xintercept = 10**-3.8)+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#d0e0ea","#4384ac", "#0a334c") %>%rev)+
  theme_classic() +
  labs(x = "Maximum Pgen retained (-log10)",
       y = "Mean distance to centroid")

```
### Ext Fig. 8c. Pgen - Cell Fate switch


```{r}
# Load cell Fate switch score ^ Pgen table 

res_pgen <- read.delim(paste0(Input_Dir, "/CellFateSwitch_Pgen.txt"))


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Plot switch score as a function of Pgen threshold
# ------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot(res_pgen,
       aes(x = -log10(threshold))) +
  geom_point(aes(y = mean_switch_Early,
                colour = "Early")) +
  geom_line(aes(y = mean_switch_Early,
                colour = "Early")) +
  geom_point(aes(y = mean_switch_Late,
                colour = "Late")) +
  geom_line(aes(y = mean_switch_Late,
                colour = "Late")) +
  
  geom_text(aes(y = mean_switch_Early,label = n_Early),    vjust = -1,    size = 3,    show.legend = FALSE  )+
  geom_text(aes(y = mean_switch_Late, label = n_Late),    vjust = -1,    size = 3,    show.legend = FALSE  )+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#C0D69F", "#61980E"))+
  theme_classic()
```



# QC
## Cell with barcodes
```{r}
dat <- data.frame(Lineag = seurat_rna$Barcode,
                  Group = seurat_rna$Group,
                  Mouse = seurat_rna$SampleBio)

dat <- dat %>%
  group_by(Mouse, Group) %>%
  summarise(
    N_Bcm = sum(!is.na(Lineag)),
    pct_barcoded = mean(!is.na(Lineag)) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"  )

df_sum <- dat %>%
  group_by(Group) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_pct = mean(pct_barcoded),
    sd_pct = sd(pct_barcoded),
    .groups = "drop"  )

p_barcode <- ggplot(df_sum,
                    aes(x = Group, y = mean_pct, fill = Group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = mean_pct - sd_pct,
        ymax = mean_pct + sd_pct),
    width = 0.2  ) +
  geom_jitter(    data = dat,
    aes(x = Group, y = pct_barcoded),
    width = 0.15,    size = 2,    alpha = 0.7,    inherit.aes = FALSE  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(values = cols_Group) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(    x = "",    y = "% cells with lineage barcode"  )

p_barcode
```


## Number of clones
```{r}
dat <- data.frame(Lineag = seurat_rna$Barcode,
                  Group = seurat_rna$Group,
                  Mouse = seurat_rna$SampleBio)

dat <- dat %>%
   filter(Mouse %in% c("8415_G2-3", "8718_D2-3", "8964_G4")) %>%
  group_by(Mouse, Group) %>%
  summarise(
    N_clones= length(unique(Lineag)),
    .groups = "drop"  )

df_sum <- dat %>%
  filter(Mouse %in% c("8415_G2-3", "8718_D2-3", "8964_G4")) %>%
  group_by(Group) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_pct = mean(N_clones),
    sd_pct = sd(N_clones),
    .groups = "drop"  )

p_barcode <- ggplot(df_sum,
                    aes(x = Group, y = mean_pct, fill = Group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = mean_pct - sd_pct,
        ymax = mean_pct + sd_pct),
    width = 0.2  ) +
  geom_jitter(    data = dat,
    aes(x = Group, y = N_clones),
    width = 0.15,    size = 2,    alpha = 0.7,    inherit.aes = FALSE  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(values = cols_Group) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(    x = "",    y = "Number of clones per gland"  )

p_barcode
```
